timing: CUDA-event interleaved (200 iters, 50 warmup) + wall-clock synced; baseline-only freeze via benchmark.py --baseline-only
correctness: PASS on B200 GPU0: 29 passed 1 skipped (fast-path test pre-native); baseline vs fp32 oracle on 4 production + 192 grid cases; 11 contract-rejection cases; NaN/wrong-eps self-tests
finding: Baseline sits ~5x above the memory-bandwidth floor (effective ~1.3-1.5 TB/s vs ~8 TB/s peak): v1 73us vs ~13.5us floor, v2 86-88us vs ~18us floor. Suspected per-row tanh re-evaluation (B*S x D = ~15.7M tanh) and/or codegen quality; NCU speed-of-light check queued before candidate design review.

target: baseline fused_norm_tanh_mul_add v1 B1 S4096 D3840 bf16 rms (profile/baseline_r0_sol/harness/run_v1.py)
finding: Baseline v1 kernel is COMPUTE-bound: SM ~67-68% vs DRAM ~24%; isolated duration 43.3us vs ~13.5us bytes-floor (3.2x device headroom). Confirms per-row tanh re-evaluation as prime lever (production scale [1,1,D] is row-invariant). Also: CUDA-event-bracketed baseline timing (73us) exceeds NCU kernel duration (43us) => CuTe tvm-ffi host path serializes inside the bracket; device-only A/B claims must therefore use NCU kernel durations, not event brackets, for the device-vs-host decomposition.
next: Codex pre-implementation review, then native candidate: hoist tanh for row-invariant scale + multi-row CTA, wide loads, fp32 reductions, exact tanhf.

correctness: PASS 33/33 on B200 GPU0 with KDA_REQUIRE_CANDIDATE=1 across all 204 native-eligible cases + edge/rejection/routing/fallback tests (240s)

benchmark.note: event channel diagnostic-only (CuTe host serializes in bracket; interleaving makes baseline event numbers unstable: 59.8-79.2us)

finding: tanh hoist works (Compute 67->32%) but 48 regs/thread halves occupancy (40% vs 81%) and K=8 cuts the grid to 512 CTAs -> kernel goes latency-bound and is SLOWER on device (52.7 vs 43.3us) despite winning end-to-end wall on v1 via the lighter host path. v2 regresses outright (two serial reductions per row at low occupancy). Next edit: __launch_bounds__ to force 4 blocks/SM (register cap), K sweep {2,4,8}.

edit: __launch_bounds__(D/8, >=4 CTAs/SM hint) register cap; KDA_ROWS_PER_CTA sweep {2,4,8}
decision: keep K=8 as default (best geomean; v2 regression fixed across all K)
evidence: benchmark.csv rows candidate-name cand-v2-lb-K{2,4,8}; NCU follow-up in profile/cand_lb_K8_sol/
finding: Register cap restored occupancy; all K beat baseline on both variants. K=8 geomean wall 1.384x (dev-event 1.316x).

edit: software-pipelined next-row x/shift(/scale) prefetch issued before current row's reduction barriers
finding: Holding 2-3 extra 16B vectors live across the CTA reduction barriers under the 32-register launch_bounds cap forces local-memory spills inside the row loop; the added local traffic dwarfs the hidden HBM latency. REJECTED; kernel reverted to the launch-bounds build (commit a9d0d4d7d). Residual gap vs bytes floor is attributed to barrier-serialized per-row reduction chains at ~1 CTA wave; further redesign (warp-per-row) out of bounded scope.
evidence: profile/cand_lb_K8_sol harness NCU run 2026-06-04; correctness suite 33/33

config: KDA_ROWS_PER_CTA=8, PDL off, no fast-math, exact tanhf
note: prefetch-chain benchmark runs earlier produced no rows: conservative busy-GPU gate aborted before writing (by design); this clean run exits 0 with full rows
evidence: benchmark.csv candidate-name cand-final-lb-K8; formal NCU reports collecting in profile/final_lb_k8_full/

edit: kSecondNorm instantiation forced to 3 CTAs/SM (~45-reg budget) to remove v2 register spills
vs_parent: 78.6 -> 93.7 us: spill relief (regs 40) did not compensate the occupancy loss (73% -> 58%)
finding: v2 spills are cheaper than reduced concurrency at this latency-bound operating point; uniform 4-CTA launch_bounds build remains FINAL. v2 ships a host-path end-to-end win (1.29x wall) over a 0.84x device kernel; the in-SGLang drop-in A/B (export step) is the decision point for shipping native v2 vs keeping the CuTe path for the second entry point.
note_benchmark_abort: benchmark aborted at start: idle gate saw the just-exited pytest process lingering in nvidia-smi (foreign_pids=[147510], util=0%); benchmark.py gains a 3x5s retry before aborting
evidence: validation run b5k62ndfy (suite + NCU); no benchmark rows written (gate abort, by design)

integration: candidate .cuh routed INSIDE existing torch.library.custom_op bodies; registration + register_fake untouched; identical wrapper layers both sides
in_sglang_correctness: PASS: 4 captured signatures vs fp32 reference through public ops + mixed-dtype fallback probe
decision: SHIP native path for BOTH entry points (v2 integrated 1.37-1.39x, parity-or-better rule satisfied); PDL off; K=8

summary: Codex completeness review REQUIRED_CHANGES addressed: arbiter rerun via export/apply_drop_in.sh (sha256 manifest; produced diff byte-identical to recorded patch), full stats incl. std captured to export/arbiter_runs/*.json, rows appended to benchmark.csv (mode in-sglang-arbiter)

method: SAME patched checkout (sglang @ edb1b3f8f + apply_drop_in.sh, diff byte-identical to export/sglang_drop_in.patch): baseline run = native routes disabled via SGLANG_NATIVE_NORM_TANH_V1=0 V2=0 (CuTe route through the identical dispatch branch); candidate run = routes enabled. Wrapper/dispatch/registration layers identical by construction.
route_decision: BOTH entry points ship natively: v1 1.617x and v2 1.404x both clear the parity-or-better rule in the dispatch-symmetric arbiter; defaults stay enabled in norm_tanh_modulation.py
in_sglang_correctness: PASS incl. strengthened fallback: native_supported(...) is False asserted for mixed-dtype probe AND fallback output matched the reference within production tolerances
sanity_vs_r2: CuTe-route medians match the clean-checkout r2 run within noise (dispatch branch cost ~nil); native-route medians match r2

gate: benchmark.py now requires REMOTE_GPU_ID matching CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, classifies start (no apps, util<=5%, mem<=2GiB, retries) and end (no foreign app) from structured nvidia-smi state; --gate-selftest covers the util=99/no-foreign counterexample; gate twice REFUSED GPU0 (foreign memory-resident app host-pid 2752289, 6.6GiB) before the move to GPU1
evidence: benchmark.csv rows candidate-name cand-final-lb-K8-gatefix-r1; negative-contract ABORT transcripts in round-1 logs
